reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 4 (Exploitability) triggered: This is a code-intrinsic behavior, not an attacker-triggered vulnerability. When a validator becomes a block proposer, it must distribute ALL 128 data columns across the network to ensure data availability, even though it only maintains custody of ~8-16 columns. This is BY DESIGN in PeerDAS: proposers have a special role in distributing data to the entire network. GossipSub allows publishing to unsubscribed topics, and the code handles failures gracefully (message_router.nim:172-177 shows error handling). The 01e property from file 01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json states 'Publishing data columns must only occur on subnets the node is subscribed to (custody/sampling)', but this doesn't account for the proposer's unique distribution responsibility. No attacker action is required to trigger this behavior; it's the node's own protocol-compliant operation. Not a security vulnerability - at most an operational consideration already handled by the implementation.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Publishing data columns must only occur on subnets the node is subscribed to (custody/sampling). [01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json:914-930]
